message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task: remove temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor, add deterministic _cache_salt helper, inject cache_salt InputField into generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signatures. Only touches dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py. Please share which file(s) your feature needs so we can confirm disjoint edits (may need to split regions within this same file if your feature also touches it).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task: add weighted tip selection (tip_weights, _select_tip_with_weights) to grounded_proposer.py. This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also touches grounded_proposer.py we need to sequence our edits carefully since it can't be split into disjoint files.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: __init__ tip_weights param, tip-selection block where set_tip_randomly is used, new _select_tip_with_weights method)
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: propose_instruction_for_predictor temperature logic, _cache_salt helper, generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signatures)
note: We both only need this one file, in different regions, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: since edits are additive/localized to different functions, let's just sequence to avoid clobbering. I'll go first (small, additive change), read the file fresh, apply my edit, then send DONE. You then read the file fresh (with my changes already in it), apply yours on top, and send DONE. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: propose_instruction_for_predictor - temperature removal + cache_salt compute/pass; new _cache_salt method; generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signature classes - add cache_salt InputField; GenerateModuleInstruction.forward - thread cache_salt through describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction calls)
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: tip_weights param/attr, _select_tip_with_weights method, and call sites where tips are currently selected - e.g. propose_instructions_for_program / propose_instruction_for_predictor tip selection logic)
note: We can't get disjoint files since both features live in grounded_proposer.py, but our edits touch different functions/regions. Proposal: I (agent1) go first since my change threads a new cache_salt kwarg through several call sites (propose_instruction_for_predictor -> instruction_generator -> GenerateModuleInstruction.forward -> describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction). I'll avoid touching tip-selection logic. Once I send DONE, please pull the file fresh and layer your tip_weights/_select_tip_with_weights changes on top, avoiding the cache_salt lines. Does that ordering work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: tip_weights param/attr in __init__, _select_tip_with_weights method, tip-selection call sites)

note: Agreed. You go first, send DONE when finished, I'll pull the file fresh and layer my changes on top avoiding your cache_salt lines.

type: ACCEPT

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: __init__ tip_weights param, tip-selection block, _select_tip_with_weights method)
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: propose_instruction_for_predictor temperature logic, _cache_salt helper, generate_instruction_class/DescribeProgram/DescribeModule signatures, GenerateModuleInstruction.forward cache_salt threading)
note: Quick clarification needed: your last message said 'go ahead and implement first' which is ambiguous about who goes first - your previous message said YOU would go first. To avoid clobbering, I will wait and NOT edit until I receive an explicit DONE from you. Please confirm: are you implementing now? If you actually want me to go first, reply explicitly 'agent2 go first' and I will proceed immediately.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished: removed temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor, added _cache_salt(pred_i, demo_set_i) method to GroundedProposer, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures, threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward and propose_instruction_for_predictor. Existing tests in tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py pass. File is ready for you to pull and layer your tip_weights/_select_tip_with_weights changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished: added tip_weights param to __init__, self.tip_weights attr, _select_tip_with_weights() helper (weighted cumulative-distribution draw via self.rng.random(), nonpositive/missing weights treated as 0, falls back to uniform self.rng.choice on all-zero weights), and updated the set_tip_randomly branch in propose_instructions_for_program to call it when tip_weights is not None. Did not touch propose_instruction_for_predictor, cache_salt, or the signature classes - that region is untouched on my end and ready for your changes if not already applied. Tests in tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py pass.
